title: Top 5 Finance Stories Today

slides.headline: Chipotle heads to Mexico
slides.explanation: The US food chain is opening its first location in Mexico. It is an interesting move since other American fast food brands have had a hard time finding success in the countries where their dishes originally came from.

slides.headline: China's robot boom
slides.explanation: Humanoid robot startups in China are racing to list on the stock market. Companies like LimX Dynamics are raising cash quickly as investors look for opportunities to cash out.

slides.headline: VW plans massive job cuts
slides.explanation: Volkswagen is considering cutting up to 100,000 jobs around the world. The company is struggling with falling profits and intense competition from Chinese car brands.

slides.headline: New UK and Swiss deal
slides.explanation: The UK and Switzerland signed a deal to remove roaming charges and let British travelers use e-gates. Officials hope this agreement will boost exports by billions over the long term.

slides.headline: Will the Fed hike rates?
slides.explanation: The chance of a July rate hike from the Federal Reserve is going up. This comes as oil prices spiked following recent events in the Strait of Hormuz.

caption: From major car industry shifts to new international trade deals, here is what is moving the markets today. Stay informed with the latest finance updates in under a minute. Tap through to see what you need to know. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: Legal battle hits Paramount deal
slides.explanation: A group of 12 states is trying to block the massive merger between Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery. They argue the deal could lead to higher prices for movies and hurt cable TV competition.

slides.headline: Concerns grow over media merger
slides.explanation: The states involved in the lawsuit claim the $110 billion deal could negatively impact movie theaters and cable providers. They say the merger might ultimately hurt regular viewers.

slides.headline: Do food apps actually work?
slides.explanation: Food tracking apps are everywhere, but it is worth asking if they really help us eat healthier. It is a debate about whether these tools are truly useful for our long-term health.

slides.headline: Waze gets an AI upgrade
slides.explanation: Waze is rolling out new features powered by Google's Gemini AI. The update is part of a larger plan to keep the app competitive against rivals like Apple Maps.

slides.headline: Microsoft cleans up Windows Search
slides.explanation: Microsoft is testing a new, simpler version of the Windows 11 search menu. It removes ads and recommended content, likely in an effort to improve the user experience.

caption: From legal battles over media giants to new AI updates on your favorite apps, here is what is happening in tech today. We are breaking down the biggest headlines to keep you informed. Swipe through for the latest updates. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
